EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A ridge of high pressure and southwest flow brought unusually warm temperatures to the Mojave Desert in the middle of the month.  High temperatures in Las Vegas rose above 95 degrees on the 9th, and remained there through the 20th.  Overnight lows were also unusually warm, including a record high minimum of 72 degrees on the 12th.  An elderly woman died of hyperthermia on the 13th.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: An 84-year old woman died of hyperthermia on May 13th in Las Vegas.  The exact time of death is unknown.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Reno Gazette-Journal reported that in a 24-hour period from about 5 p.m. Thursday [Jan. 4th], when heavy snow began falling, until Friday, Reno police said 95 accidents were reported.  In a rollover automobile accident a University of Nevada-Reno journalism professor was killed.  Also, a five-car crash on U.S. Highway 395 south of Reno slowed traffic for three hours.  In this crash two people were injured and sent to the hospital.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Hot weather across western Nevada during the first two weeks of July caused record high temperatures, and record warm minimum temperatures, at many locations.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Record warm temperatures occurred across western Nevada with Reno setting a new record high for the date of 106 on the 4th.  Reno tied its all-time record high temperature of 108 degrees on July 5th.  Carson City set new record high temperatures for two dates with 102 on July 5th and 104 on the 6th.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Hot weather across western Nevada during the first two weeks of July caused record high temperatures, and record warm minimum temperatures, at many locations.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Record warm temperatures occurred in Nevada's Basin and Range in early July.  Lovelock recorded daily high temperatures of 100 degrees or greater for eight consecutive days from July 3rd through July 10th.  The temperature rose to 108 degrees on the 4th, 112 on the 5th, and 110 on the 6th.  It reached 109 degrees on the 10th.  New record highs were set from July 4th through the 10th.  The 112 degrees which occurred on the 5th set a new all-time record high for Lovelock.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: Lightning struck the Mark Twain Fire Station building in Dayton.  The building was severely damaged.  A 1952 International fire engine that was being stored in the building was also damaged.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: A trained weather spotter located 4 miles SSW of Fallon reported a thunderstorm wind gust of 61 knots (70 mph).

EVENT_NARRATIVE: A trained weather spotter located on U.S. Highway 50 ten miles southeast of Salt Wells reported strong thunderstorm wind gusts which knocked down several large trees along with a power pole.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A late-season surge of monsoon moisture was already in place when the remnant circulation of former Atlantic Hurricane Dean moved over southern Nevada during the pre-dawn hours of August 27th, resulting in major flooding.  Damage figures were not available at this time, but will likely run well into the millions.  In addition, there were 22 swift water rescues in the Las Vegas Valley during the height of the flooding.  Despite the magnitude of the flooding, no deaths or serious injuries were reported.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Flash flooding resulted in 22 swift water rescues in less than three hours.  Floodwaters knocked down a 45-foot stretch of stucco wall behind an apartment complex, inundating garages with two feet of water.  Many homes had water in them.  The damage figure is an estimate.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A powerful winter storm brought strong winds and over three feet of snow to the eastern Sierra Nevada and Carson Range. Strong winds also occurred in western Nevada.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavenly Ski Resort reported a 2-day storm total of 48 inches of snowfall at an elevation of 9100 feet.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A powerful winter storm brought strong winds and over three feet of snow to the eastern Sierra Nevada and Carson Range. Strong winds also occurred in western Nevada.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Carson Valley NDOT wind sensor 3 miles north of Minden recorded a 51 knot (59 mph) wind gust.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A powerful winter storm brought strong winds and over three feet of snow to the eastern Sierra Nevada and Carson Range. Strong winds also occurred in western Nevada.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Washoe Valley NDOT wind sensor 2 miles south of New Washoe City recorded a 54 knot (62 mph) wind gust.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A powerful winter storm brought strong winds and over three feet of snow to the eastern Sierra Nevada and Carson Range. Strong winds also occurred in western Nevada.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The NDOT wind sensor at Walker Lake recorded a 75 knot (86 mph) wind gust. Other wind gusts of over 60 mph were recorded between 2:15 am and 4:45 am.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A powerful winter storm brought strong winds and over three feet of snow to the eastern Sierra Nevada and Carson Range. Strong winds also occurred in western Nevada.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A total of 6 inches of snow fell 10 miles northwest of Reno at an elevation of 5360 feet.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: One day after a powerful snowstorm moved through the region an avalanche occurred at the Mt. Rose ski area.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: While perfoming routine safety work a ski patroller was caught in an avalanche in the Chutes area of Mt. Rose ski area.  The ski patroller was injured and was airlifted off the mountain.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A winter storm brought heavy snow and strong winds to the eastern Sierra Nevada. Strong winds, but smaller snowfall totals, were also reported in western Nevada.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A weather spotter 4 miles north of South Lake Tahoe (near Stateline) reported a total of 12 inches of snowfall.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A winter storm brought heavy snow and strong winds to the eastern Sierra Nevada. Strong winds, but smaller snowfall totals, were also reported in western Nevada.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A 58 knot (67 mph) wind gust was reported 5 miles north of Hawthorne.  The Mineral County sheriff that a semi-truck was blown over by the strong winds near Walker Lake.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Severe downburst winds blowing over a burn area created a dust storm around Winnemucca.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Winds gusting to 60 mph were reported by an Incident Meteorologist assigned to a fire 2 miles south of Winnemucca. The ASOS at the Winnemucca airport measured a gust to 59 mph.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Severe downburst winds blowing over a burn area created a dust storm around Winnemucca.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Downburst winds from a severe thunderstorm blowing over a recently burned area 2 miles south of Winnemucca picked up ash and dust reducing visibilities to 1/4 mile or less across portions of Winnemucca and the surrounding area. The Incident Meteorlogist at the Thomas Fire about 2 miles south of Winnemucca reported a severe dust storm and the ASOS at the airport reported visibilities down to 1/4 mile.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A line of severe thunderstorms with wind gusts up to 62 mph created a duststorm that reduced visibilities to 1/4 mile or less from around Elko to near Beowawe.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A line of severe thunderstorms moving from southwest to northeast across southwest Elko county produced a wind gust to 62 mph at the Weather Forecast Office in Elko and a estimated gust to 60 mph at Deeth.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A line of severe thunderstorms with wind gusts up to 62 mph created a duststorm that reduced visibilities to 1/4 mile or less from around Elko to near Beowawe.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A line of severe thunderstorms moving from southwest to northeast across southwest Elko county created a duststorm which reduced visibilities to 1/4 mile or less in Elko and the surrounding area.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A line of severe thunderstorms with wind gusts up to 62 mph created a duststorm that reduced visibilities to 1/4 mile or less from around Elko to near Beowawe.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Outflow winds from a line of thunderstorms picked up dust and ash from a recent burn area south of Interstate 80 which caused visibility to drop to near zero near the Beowawe ramps. This led to 2 auto accidents sending 1 person to the hospital with moderate head injuries.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A line of severe thunderstorms moved across eastern Elko county.
A line of severe thunderstorms moved across eastern Elko county.
Very heavy rainfall caused standing water and flash flooding across portions of far western Nevada.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Very heavy rainfall caused flash flooding in the Lockwood area.  A Washoe County official reported 8 inches of standing water on roads near Exit 22 on Interstate 80.  At other locations in the area up to a foot of water was running across roads.  A fire department chief described areas of water as small lakes.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A late-season cold front moved through the Sierra and western Nevada on June 5th.  Strong winds accompanied the front and caused damage mainly in western Nevada.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A trained weather spotter in Gardnerville reported a 55 kt (63 mph) wind gust.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A late-season cold front moved through the Sierra and western Nevada on June 5th.  Strong winds accompanied the front and caused damage mainly in western Nevada.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A 51 kt (59 mph) wind gust was recorded by the Desert Springs RAWS sensor at an elevation of 5280 feet.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Heavy rainfall occurred across the eastern Sierra and western Nevada.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Strong thunderstorm outflow winds caused a dust storm in western Pershing County.  Winds were estimated at between 40 and 50 mph.  Visibility was reduced to less than 100 feet.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A winter storm brought heavy snowfall to the Sierra Nevada and snow and heavy rainfall to western Nevada.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Storm-total snowfall amounts:||Diamond Peak Ski Resort, 24 inches (at 8500 feet), and 12 inches at 6700 feet;|Mt. Rose ski area, 20 inches;|Daggett Pass, 16 inches;|Glenbrook, 8 inches.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An area of low pressure and associated cold front brought heavy snow to the Sierra Nevada and strong winds to western Nevada.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Storm-total snowfall amounts:||Mt. Rose ski area, 15 inches;|Heavenly Ski Resort, 10 inches;|one mile NE of Incline Village, 10 inches.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Heavy rains during the late afternoon and early evening of August 1 caused flash flooding in and around Eureka.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy rains on the hilly terrain in and around Eureka produced large amounts of runoff that damaged about a dozen homes and a few businesses. One home had 4 feet of mud in the basement. Many washouts occurred on rural roads. Flooding was also reported across state highway 278 about 7 miles north of Eureka. The Eureka county airport reported 1.63 inches of rain but the radar indicated the heaviest rain occurred just west of downtown Eureka with radar estimates of 2 to 3 inches.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An area of low pressure and associated cold front brought heavy snow to the Sierra Nevada and strong winds to western Nevada.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A cooperative weather observer reported 5 inches of snowfall in Virginia City.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A late-season cold front moved through the Sierra and western Nevada on June 5th.  Strong winds accompanied the front and caused damage mainly in western Nevada.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A 70 kt (80 mph) wind gust was recorded by the Galena RAWS sensor at an elevation of 5610 feet.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A late-season cold front moved through the Sierra and western Nevada on June 5th.  Strong winds accompanied the front and caused damage mainly in western Nevada.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A 56 kt (64 mph) wind gust was recorded at the NDOT wind sensor at Holbrook Junction at an elevation of 5357 feet.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A late-season cold front moved through the Sierra and western Nevada on June 5th.  Strong winds accompanied the front and caused damage mainly in western Nevada.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A trained spotter reported a dust storm in the Spanish Springs area five miles northeast of Reno.  Blowing dust was reducing visibility to near zero in the vicinity of La Posada Blvd.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A late-season cold front moved through the Sierra and western Nevada on June 5th.  Strong winds accompanied the front and caused damage mainly in western Nevada.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Nevada Appeal newspaper reported that strong wind gusts brought down tree limbs in Carson City.  One downed tree limb on Fifth Street knocked out power to 900 residents, including the Carson City Courthouse and Sheriff's Department.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A winter storm brought heavy snowfall to the Sierra Nevada and snow and heavy rainfall to western Nevada.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Storm-total snowfall amounts:||Virginia City, 8 inches;|Golden Valley area 5 miles north of Reno, 6 inches;|northwest Reno foothills, 6 inches;|southwest Reno foothills, 5 inches;|4 miles SSW of Reno, 4 inches.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: The second of two early season storms brought snow to the Sierra and strong winds to western Nevada.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The ASOS sensor at the Reno-Tahoe International Airport recorded a wind gust of 52 knots (60 mph).

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: The second of two early season storms brought snow to the Sierra and strong winds to western Nevada.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The wind sensor at the Reno NWS Forecast Office recorded a wind gust of 57 knots (66 mph).

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: The second of two early season storms brought snow to the Sierra and strong winds to western Nevada.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The ASOS sensor at the Reno-Tahoe International Airport recorded a wind gust of 53 knots (61 mph).

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A Pacific storm system brought strong southwest winds to portions of the Mojave Desert and Southern Great Basin.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Emergency Services communications tower blown down in Pahrump, NV.  Measured gust to 61 mph occurred at KDRA ASOS approximately 75 minutes after the tower was blown down.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Subtropical moisture over the Desert Southwest fueled thunderstorms which brought heavy rain and flooding.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Three motorists were rescued after urban flooding covered both Warm Springs Rd. and Boulder Highway.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Subtropical moisture over the Desert Southwest fueled thunderstorms which brought heavy rain and flooding.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: State Route 159 was covered with mud and rocks west of Interstate 215.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong low pressure system brought thunderstorms and heavy rain to the Mojave Desert.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Several roads were washed out in Pahrump.  The co-op observer reported 2.7 inches of rain for the day.  Time of damage occurrence unknown.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong low pressure system brought thunderstorms and heavy rain to the Mojave Desert.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Mount Charleston had flooding and debris over roads, with a couple of subdivisions blocked off.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A late-season surge of monsoon moisture was already in place when the remnant circulation of former Atlantic Hurricane Dean moved over southern Nevada during the pre-dawn hours of August 27th, resulting in major flooding.  Damage figures were not available at this time, but will likely run well into the millions.  In addition, there were 22 swift water rescues in the Las Vegas Valley during the height of the flooding.  Despite the magnitude of the flooding, no deaths or serious injuries were reported.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The same storms which were responsible for major flooding also produced lightning strikes which set fire to at least ten buildings and more than 20 trees.  Damage figure is an estimate.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong low pressure system brought thunderstorms and heavy rain to the Mojave Desert.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Lightning set fire to an office complex at Eastern Ave. and Horizon Ridge Pkwy.
